title: Mod Modular Office Bookcase
summary: An asymmetric bookcase — one open end, one closed end with a small secondary shelf — that appears to float above the floor on a recessed base.
description:
This bookcase reads as more architectural than most shelf builds because of two details: one end is closed off with its own small shelf and trim, breaking up what would otherwise be one long open box, and the whole thing sits on a recessed 2x2 base that's set back from the outer edges so the case appears to float slightly above the floor.

Decide up front whether your shelves will be fixed or adjustable on shelf pins — both options are given at each shelf step, and it's worth choosing once for the whole piece rather than mixing approaches, since a fixed shelf also serves as extra structural bracing that an adjustable one doesn't.

tools.note: Rips the plywood/MDF into consistent 15-1/2"-wide strips.
For shelf-pin holes, if going the adjustable route.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood (for staining) or MDF (for painting), ripped 15-1/2" wide, 8 ft sheet
Edge banding
materials.note: Only needed if staining rather than painting.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Measure and mark all the joints on every board. If using a pocket-hole jig, drill 1-1/4" pocket holes; if using 2" wood screws, predrill. Add glue and screw together.
materials: 3/4" plywood (for staining) or MDF (for painting), ripped 15-1/2" wide, 8 ft sheet

steps.title: Install the large shelf
steps.body: Decide between shelf pins (drill holes matched to your pins for an adjustable shelf) or a fixed shelf attached with glue and screws the same way as the box.
materials: 3/4" plywood (for staining) or MDF (for painting), ripped 15-1/2" wide, 8 ft sheet

steps.title: Attach the small shelf sides
steps.body: Use 2" finish nails and glue to attach the small shelf sides to the open end of the bookshelf, leaving 3/4" of clearance under the bottom shelf.

steps.title: Add the small shelf
steps.body: Add the small shelf with screws and glue for a fixed shelf, or drill shelf-pin holes for an adjustable one.
materials: 3/4" plywood (for staining) or MDF (for painting), ripped 15-1/2" wide, 8 ft sheet

steps.title: Attach the closed end trim
steps.body: Attach the closed end trim to the closed end (and to the shelf too, if it's not fixed). Use 2" finish nails and glue. If using a pocket-hole jig, build the face frame separately first, then nail the whole frame on as one piece.

steps.title: Attach the small bookshelf trim
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom trim to the small bookshelf section the same way — build the face frame separately if using a jig, then attach as one piece with 2" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the small shelf side trim
steps.body: Attach the small shelf side trim with 2" finish nails and glue. If your shelves are fixed, also nail into them here for added support.

steps.title: Build the floating base
steps.body: Use 2" screws and glue to attach the floating base sides to the bottom, then attach the floating base ends to finish the base — set back from the outer case edges so the bookcase reads as slightly elevated off the floor.

images.alt: Modern bookcase with a closed end, small secondary shelf, and floating base
